question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence Prometheus metric labels inject a PromQL-breaking cluster name (e.g., containing `|`, `(`, `)`, `{`, `}`) into the `clusterRegex` via `clusterRegexOrNull()`, causing `PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY.formatted(properties.getClusterRegex())` to produce a malformed PromQL query that permanently returns no series, forcing `evaluate()` to always return `up()` and masking real importer lag, thereby permanently freezing fund-related transaction monitoring?
